commands.review_summary:
This PR changes Deep Research browser completion to prefer page-scoped CDP target extraction for OOPIF reports, tags session-bound Chrome clients with their page session id, and adds regression tests plus changelog coverage.

Reproducibility: yes. The base-main source is enough to reproduce the dispatch bug: production passes both Page and client, but the old code takes the Page path first and cannot reach OOPIF-only reports; the PR body also includes real reattach output for the failure and fix.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No repair lane is needed; the remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the scoped CDP target extraction and release timing for the browser fix.

target.title: fix(browser): extract Deep Research report from out-of-process iframe
target.body:
## Problem

Deep Research runs via the browser engine stopped returning results. The session hangs until timeout (or stays stuck `running`), and harvest captures only the `"ChatGPT said:"` placeholder instead of the report.

## Root cause

ChatGPT moved the Deep Research report out of the inline assistant turn and into a **doubly-nested, out-of-process sandboxed iframe**:

```
chatgpt.com/c/…                            → assistantCount: 0  (no report in the main DOM)
 └─ connector_openai_deep_research (OOPIF)  → body empty (sandbox shell)
     └─ nested same-origin iframe           → the actual report ("Research completed in …")
```

`waitForDeepResearchCompletion` has two extraction paths:

- `readDeepResearchFrameResult` — reads the **main page's** frame tree via isolated worlds. The OOPIF does **not** appear in the main page's frame tree, so `findDeepResearchFrameId` returns `null`. It can never see the report.
- `readDeepResearchTargetResult` — attaches to the iframe's own CDP target and walks its nested frames. This one **can** read the report.

The dispatch was `Page ? frameResult : client ? targetResult : null`. In production **both `Page` and `client` are always passed** (see `index.ts` and `reattach.ts`), so it always took the broken frame path and the working target path was effectively dead code. The completion poll never saw `finished` (no assistant turn in the main DOM either), so the run timed out.

A second guard (`hasActiveScopedResearch`) also depended on a main-DOM assistant turn that no longer exists, which would have blocked the report even after fixing dispatch.

## Fix

- Prefer the target-attach path (reaches the OOPIF + nested frames); fall back to the in-page frame path for legacy/inline rendering.
- Treat a **target-confirmed** completion as authoritative even when the main DOM exposes no assistant turn, since the report now lives entirely in the OOPIF.

The scoped-staleness guard is preserved for the in-page frame path (target-confirmed completions read the live connector iframe directly, so they don't need the main-DOM corroboration).

## Verification

Reattaching to a real completed Deep Research session now works end-to-end through the actual CLI:

```
Monitoring Deep Research (timeout: 40min)...
Deep Research completed (6s elapsed)
[browser] Saved deep-research-report artifact … deep-research-report.md (26.7 KB)
Reattach succeeded; session marked completed.
```

Previously this same flow extracted only `"ChatGPT said:"` and ran to timeout.

## Tests

- Adds a regression test: production shape (both `Page` and `client` passed, scoped run, `minTurnIndex >= 0`) where the report is only reachable via the target path and the main DOM has no assistant turn → returns the report instead of hanging.
- Existing staleness-protection test (`does not complete from an unscoped frame result during a scoped run`) still passes — the guard relaxation only applies to target-confirmed completions.
- `deepResearch.test.ts`: 28 passed. Full browser suite: 399 passed / 1 skipped.

## Known follow-up (not in this PR)

`readDeepResearchTargetResult` enumerates targets browser-wide (`Target.getTargets`). With multiple ChatGPT tabs open in the persistent profile, each holding a completed Deep Research report, a run could in principle pick up another tab's report. A follow-up should scope target enumeration to the current page session (the `client` is already connected to the page target) and drop the browser-wide scan.

---

## Follow-up: cross-tab scoping + wrapper-path binding + fresh live proof

The first review flagged that target-first extraction scanned browser-wide CDP targets (cross-tab leak). Addressed in two commits:

- **`53b7b331`** — scope discovery to the current page via page-session auto-attach; drop the browser-wide `Target.getTargets`/`attachToTarget` scan.
- **`95903b61`** — the page-scoping only held for direct-tab clients. On the browser-WSEndpoint path, `client` is a session-bound wrapper (`createSessionBoundChromeClient`) whose raw `send` is browser-level, so `Target.setAutoAttach` via `send` still went browser-wide there. Now the wrapper is tagged with its page session id (`oraclePageSessionId`) and auto-attach is bound to it explicitly. The cross-tab boundary now holds on both connection paths.

**Regression tests** (both verified to FAIL against the pre-fix source):
- foreign completed Deep Research target is never read (page-scoped discovery)
- every `Target.setAutoAttach` on a session-bound wrapper client is bound to the page session

**Fresh live multi-tab proof (latest head `95903b61`)** — a signed-in Chrome with **two** completed Deep Research tabs open; the real `waitForDeepResearchCompletion` run scoped to each tab returns that tab's own report, no cross-contamination:

```jsonc
{
  "tabA": { "name": "v2 (Routing Cost Map)",  "len": 27099,
            "head": "Current LLM Inference Routing Cost Map / Scope and reading notes ...",
            "hasOwn_RoutingMap": true,  "leaked_fromB": false },
  "tabB": { "name": "v1 (Provider Cost Map)", "len": 14317,
            "head": "LLM Inference Provider Cost Map / Scope and normalization ...",
            "hasOwn_ProviderMap": true, "leaked_fromA": false }
}
// ISOLATION PASS — each tab returned its OWN report, no cross-contamination
```

Both connector OOPIFs (`connector_openai_deep_research.*`) were present simultaneously; tab A extracted the Routing report and tab B the Provider report, each from its own page-scoped session.

**Verification**: `tests/browser/deepResearch.test.ts` 30 passed; full browser suite 401 passed / 1 skipped; typecheck (changed files) / format / lint clean.

commands.review_summary:
The branch hardens plugin and Codex schema, metadata, registry, provider/channel, setup, and trusted-tool-policy projection paths against unreadable or malformed plugin-owned data.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main still directly reads plugin-owned policy and schema fields, and the PR adds synthetic unreadable-entry tests for the affected paths. I did not run the PR tests in this read-only review.

target.title: fix(plugin): harden schema and metadata fuzz boundaries
target.body:
## Summary

- hardens plugin-owned schema and metadata boundaries found during fuzzing with synthetic `fuzzplugin` / `mockplugin` fixtures
- latest refresh adds bounded trusted-tool-policy projection: unreadable policy registry rows and unreadable policy bodies now fail closed, while before-tool-call diagnostics reuse the same safe projection instead of crashing on hostile policy ids
- branch already covers agent tool-result middleware discovery, Codex app-server extension factory discovery, channel setup rows, provider discovery fallbacks beside runtime manifest catalogs, loader cache-key metadata, plugin registry alias reads, provider tool schema arrays, configured model catalog API reads, and recent mainline conflict surfaces
- current head is `f43dc68a6738a62528b7870b8bdacbfc322dc629`
- final focused proof ran after a clean rebase on `origin/main` at `adf981de896ace57f2e249562fbcf9580d099e87`

## Verification

- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts --reporter=verbose -t "trusted policy registry row is unreadable|trusted policy registration is unreadable"`: 1 file passed, 2 tests passed
- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.embedded-mode.test.ts --reporter=verbose -t "projects trusted policy diagnostics"`: 2 files passed, 2 tests passed
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/plugins/trusted-tool-policy.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.ts src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.embedded-mode.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/plugins/trusted-tool-policy.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.ts src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.embedded-mode.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- branch diff, PR body, and spec scrubbed for the reported plugin name and private local paths: clean
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`: clean, no accepted/actionable findings after the trusted-policy patch; the one accepted finding caught a fail-open unreadable-row case, which is now fixed and covered by a regression
- previous refreshed agent tool-result middleware proof: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/agent-tool-result-middleware.test.ts --reporter=verbose` passed 1 file, 7 tests
- previous refreshed Codex app-server factory proof: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/codex-app-server-extension-factory.test.ts --reporter=verbose` passed 1 file, 2 tests
- previous refreshed shard proof remains relevant for the broader stack: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/loader.runtime-registry.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-tools.test.ts src/agents/schema/clean-for-gemini.test.ts src/plugins/plugin-registry.test.ts src/plugins/channel-plugin-ids.test.ts src/plugins/provider-discovery.runtime.test.ts src/flows/channel-setup.status.test.ts --reporter=verbose` passed 2 Vitest shards; 3 unit-fast files / 51 tests and 4 plugin files / 195 tests
- `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`: blocked before remote execution because Blacksmith is not authenticated (`blacksmith auth login` required)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: plugin-owned schema, metadata, command metadata, hook registration, tool registration options, CLI registration options, bundled agent tool result middleware options, captured middleware options, captured capability id surfaces, provider/runtime/channel/plugin registry projections, model/provider install catalog projections, provider discovery projection, web search install catalog projection, model picker provider catalog projection, web search setup provider projection, provider model-picker flow projection, provider setup flow projection, provider runtime hook-ref projection, channel setup selection projection, configured model catalog projection, Codex app-server extension factory discovery, agent tool-result middleware discovery, and trusted tool policy projection no longer trust crashy getters, revoked proxies, mutable arrays/records, malformed runtime entries, malformed ids, unreadable aliases, unreadable hook aliases, unreadable env vars, unreadable install/provider metadata, unreadable catalog hooks, unreadable setup provider rows, unreadable setup choices, unreadable channel setup rows, unreadable model-picker contribution rows, unreadable configured model API rows, unreadable Codex extension factory entries, unreadable middleware runtime/handler entries, unreadable policy rows, or unreadable policy ids while healthy sibling registrations continue where the surface is availability-oriented and security-sensitive policy/scope failures fail closed.

Real environment tested: local linked OpenClaw worktree on the draft PR branch, current head `f43dc68a6738a62528b7870b8bdacbfc322dc629`, rebased onto `origin/main` at `adf981de896ace57f2e249562fbcf9580d099e87`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: rebased cleanly onto current `origin/main`, ran the focused trusted-policy and before-tool-call diagnostic Vitest commands, ran formatting, targeted linting, commit diff check, private-name scrub, autoreview, and attempted the Blacksmith-backed changed gate listed in Verification.

Evidence after fix: `host-hooks.contract.test.ts` now seeds both an unreadable trusted policy registration and an unreadable trusted policy registry row while proving execution blocks deterministically instead of silently skipping policy enforcement. `agent-tools.before-tool-call.embedded-mode.test.ts` proves diagnostics survive a hostile policy id getter and still include the healthy `mockplugin` policy entry. Existing focused coverage still proves the broader stack around provider discovery, channel setup selection, plugin registry projections, provider tool schema normalization, configured model catalog projection, Codex app-server factory discovery, and middleware discovery.

Observed result after fix: malformed trusted policy registry entries no longer crash diagnostics or fail open by disappearing from the policy list; runtime policy execution blocks with a deterministic reason when policy enforcement data is unreadable.

What was not tested: broad Blacksmith/Testbox changed gate did not run because the local Blacksmith CLI is unauthenticated (`blacksmith auth login` required). AWS Crabbox broad gate was not rerun for this refresh; current proof is the targeted local slice plus formatter/lint/diff/scrub/autoreview checks.
